briefdescription.para: CFRAME Driver Instance.
This function reads bootheader and metaheader and validates them.

parameterdescription.para: is instance pointer pointing to PDI details
is the value of the Multiboot register
is the PDI address

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on success.
XLOADER_ERR_IMGHDR_TBL if PLM is unable to read image header table.
XLOADER_ERR_MEMSET_SECURE_PTR if failed to create instance for secure pointer.
XLOADER_ERR_UNSUPPORTED_PDI_VER on PDI version used in secure operations is unsupported.
XLOADER_ERR_SECURE_NOT_ENABLED on attempt to secure boot in an excluded secure critical code.
XLOADER_ERR_GEN_IDCODE on ID code mismatch.
XLOADER_ERR_IMGHDR if image header checksum fails.
XLOADER_ERR_PRTNHDR if partition header checksum fails.
XLOADER_ERR_SECURE_METAHDR if secure metaheader validation fails.

inbodydescription.para: Read Boot header
Read meta header from PDI source
Update the flash offset address in PDI instance if the boot mode is QSPI, OSPI or SD_RAW.
Read image header table from Meta Header
Check the validity of Img Hdr Table fields
Update the PDI ID in the RTC area of PMC RAM
Read the IHT and Optional data from Metaheader
Update KEK red key availability status if PLM is encrypted with Black key
Read Image Header Table to know if authentication is enabled for Meta header
Validate if authentication/encryption is compulsory
Authenticate Image Header Table, this is applicable for only Versal and VersalNet platforms. For Versal_2ve_2vm platform whole MetaHeader is authenticated with HashBlock signature.
Perform IDCODE and Extended IDCODE checks
Read and verify image headers and partition headers

briefdescription.para: This function is used to load and start images.

detaileddescription.para: It reads meta header, loads the images as present in the PDI and starts images based on hand-off information present in PDI.

parameterdescription.para: is Pdi instance pointer

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on success.
XLOADER_ERR_DELAY_ATTRB if both delay handoff and delay load are set for the same image.
XLOADER_ERR_NUM_HANDOFF_CPUS if number of CPUs exceed max count.

inbodydescription.para: From the meta header present in PDI pointer, read the subsystem image details and load, start all the images

For every image,
Read the CDO file if present
Send the CDO file to cdo parser which directs CDO commands to Xilpm, and other components
Load partitions to respective memories
Enable Ssit Events once the basic NoC has been configured in SSIT devices
Release boot device once image loading completes or on error
Update with current status if no failures observed before
Execute delayed handoff after the boot device is released
Only update handoff params for ELF partitions. CDO/data partitions may have DstnCpu set for ownership but should not trigger CPU wakeup.

Check if the IdCode read is VC1902 ES1
Check if a subset of checks to be bypassed

briefdescription.para: This function calculates hash and compares with expected hash.

para.verbatim: Hash is calculated on AC + Data for first block, encrypts the ECDSA/RSA signature and compares it with the expected hash. After first block, hash is calculated on block of data and compared with the expected hash.

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the XLoader_SecureParams instance
is size of the data block to be processed which includes padding lengths and hash
notifies if the block to be processed is last or not

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on success.
XLOADER_ERR_PRTN_HASH_CALC_FAIL if hash calculation fails for partition authentication.
XLOADER_ERR_PRTN_AUTH_FAIL if partition authentication fails.
XLOADER_ERR_PRTN_HASH_COMPARE_FAIL if failed to compare partition hash.
XLOADER_SEC_BUF_CLEAR_ERR if failed to clear buffer.
Hash should be calculated on AC + first chunk
Verify the hash
Skip sign verification, compare the hash of respective partition which uses same keys.
Authenticate the signature, if same keys are not used.
Update the next expected hash and data location
Remove the hash length at end of the chunk

briefdescription.para: This function validates the encryption keysrc, puf helper data location and eFUSE IV for ENC only case.

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the XLoader_SecureParams instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on success.
XLOADER_SEC_ENC_ONLY_KEYSRC_ERR if key source is not from efuse black key when encrypt only is enabled.
XLOADER_SEC_ENC_ONLY_PUFHD_LOC_ERR if PUFHD location is from eFuse when encrypt only is enabled.
If encrypt only is enabled, verify that key source is from efuse black key. Otherwise return XLOADER_SEC_ENC_ONLY_KEYSRC_ERR.
If encrypt only is enabled, verify that PUF HD is from efuse. Otherwise return XLOADER_SEC_ENC_ONLY_PUFHD_LOC_ERR.
Validate MetaHdr IV range with eFUSE IV

briefdescription.para: IV Criteria check.

detaileddescription.para:
IHPtr
is the IV to be compared
EfusePtr
is the eFUSE cache address
XST_SUCCESS on success.
XLOADER_SEC_IV_METAHDR_RANGE_ERROR on Failure.
Example: iv[95:0] - F7F8FDE0 8674A28D C6ED8E37 Bootgen follows the big-endian format so the values stored will be
Our xilnvm driver also follows the same format to store it in eFUSE
EfusePtr[0]=C6ED8E37 -> IV[31:0] EfusePtr[1]=8674A28D -> IV[63:32] EfusePtr[2]=F7F8FDE0 -> IV[95:64]]
Spec says: IV[95:32] defined by user in meta header should match with eFUSEIV[95:32] IV[31:0] defined by user in meta header should >= eFUSEIV[31:0]

briefdescription.para: This function clears the AES PUF,RED and expanded keys.

Run AES Kat test if the image is encrypted
Check secure state of boot as partition is allowed to be encrypted only if Secure state of boot is S-HWRoT, Emul S-HWRoT, A-HWRoT or Emul A-HWRoT.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes XLoader_SecureParams's instance.

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the XLoader_SecureParams instance.
is pointer to the XilPdi instance
is the partition number to be processed

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on success.
XLOADER_ERR_MEMSET_SECURE_PTR if failed to set memory for secure pointer.
XLOADER_ERR_INIT_GET_DMA if failed to get DMA instance during initialization.
Initialize XLoader_SecureParms instance with zeros.
Initialize XLoader_SecureTempParams instance with zeros.
Read partition header from meta header.

The following initialization is crucial as the authentication certificate and PUF data are now stored in the PMC RAM instead of the PPU1 RAM. During the processing of the first chunk of any partition, the second 32K chunk of the PMC RAM, starting from 0xf2008120, contains the authentication certificate and PUF data as applicable. As a result, the second chunk of the partition must be loaded at 0xf2000020, not at 0xf2008120. Therefore, double buffering is disabled when processing the first chunk, and only enabled from the second chunk onwards. The third chunk is loaded at 0xf2008120, and from then on, the chunks are loaded alternatively to the two 32KB chunks of the PMC RAM.
Get DMA instance
Verify revoke ID if encryption is enabled
Initialize the checksum.
Initialize the authentication.
Initialize the encryption.

memberdef.name: XLoader_SecureChunkCopy

briefdescription.para: This function copies the data from SrcAddr to chunk memory during processing of secure partitions.

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the XLoader_SecureParams instance
is the source address from which the data is to be processed or copied
notifies if the block to be processed is last or not
is size of the data block to be processed which doesn't include padding lengths and hash.
is pointer to TotalSize which has to be processed

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on success.
XLOADER_ERR_DATA_COPY_FAIL if failed to copy data to PMC RAM.
Copy the next chunk securely, and then wait for the process to be completed.

The following initialization is crucial as the authentication certificate and PUF data are now stored in the PMC RAM instead of the PPU1 RAM. During the processing of the first chunk of any partition, the second 32K chunk of the PMC RAM, starting from 0xf2008120, contains the authentication certificate and PUF data as applicable. As a result, the second chunk of the partition must be loaded at 0xf2000020, not at 0xf2008120. Therefore, double buffering is disabled when processing the first chunk, and only enabled from the second chunk onwards. The third chunk is loaded at 0xf2008120, and from then on, the chunks are loaded alternatively to the two 32KB chunks of the PMC RAM.

XPLMI_RTCFG_SECURESTATE_EMUL_SHWROT - PLM is encrypted

briefdescription.para: This function reads the value of PPK efuse bits, DEC only efuse bits and fields in bootheader and accordingly sets the Secure State of boot.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on SUCCESS and error code in case of failure.

simplesect.para: The Secure State of the device will be stored in two 32-bit registers in RTC area of PMCRAM and two global variables -one for authenticated boot and other for encrypted boot, for redundancy.

listitem.para: Check secure state for authentication
Set the secure state for authentication in register and global variable.
Check secure state for encryption.
Read DEC_ONLY efuse bits.
If more than one DEC_ONLY efuse bits are programmed, then set the SHWRoT register.
If none of the DEC_ONLY efuse bits are programmed and PLM is encrypted, then set the SHWRoT register.
If none of the DEC_ONLY efuse bits are programmed and PLM is not encrypted, then set the SHWRoT register.

briefdescription.para: This function loads the ASU firmware image from DDR during In-Place ASU Update.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on success.
Error code on failure

inbodydescription.para: Memset the PDI instance.
Initialize the PDI to load the ASU subsystem from DDR.
Check if ASU is present in InPlace update PDI and get the Image number and Partition number. If ASU is not found, return error.
Load the ASU image.
We need to reset NoOfHandOffCpus here because we don't call StartImage routine
Signal ASUFW that ELF has been loaded.

briefdescription.para: This function will load the LPD and PSM ELF file from DDR.

Initialize the PDI to load the LPD subsystem from DDR.

Load the LPD image.
We need to reset NoOfHandOffCpus here because we don't call StartImage routine
